rulings.Case Title: 5810-5816-5818 MISSION STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, VS. ANGELA D WARREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft'S Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Statement Of Damages And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 6, 2013, Line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT 5810-5816-5818 MISSION STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION Motion To Compel Defendant's Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Statement Of Damages And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Defendant Angela Warren is to provide Responses to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production of Documents without objections. Defendant Warren is also to provide responsive documents and a Response to the Request for Statement of Damages. The Court orders Counsel to pay sanctions of $1020.00 to Plaintiff. Payment, responses and responsive documents are due within 15 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT A. WASHINGTON VS. SELF STORAGE ADVANTAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 6, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT RANDY MCBRIDE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Hearing required. The Court is inclined to grant the motion because Defendant has demonstrated that he is a good faith purchaser pursuant to Business & Professions Code §21711 and not liable. Further, Defendant has met his burden of showing for each cause of action that one or more elements of the cause of action cannot be established, and Plaintiff has not met his burden of making a prima facie showing with admissible evidence of the existence of a triable issue of material fact. Defendant has shown he did not enter into a contract with Plaintiff, that he did not make any representations to Plaintiff, that he did not call the police, that he does not know Defendants Liebowitz, Gardner, or any employees or agents of Self-Storage. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE KILBY BLACK VS. DENNIS DOYLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 6, 2013, Line 4. DEFENDANTS MORSE FAMILY TRUST, FREDRICK MORSE'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court notes that Defendant violated California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1113(d). The Court could not find any request for a longer memorandum on file. Moreover, Defendant violated California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1345 as the Separate Statement did not contain a complete statement of the Requests for Production of Documents and the responses thereto. The Court urges counsel to comply with these Court rules in the future. Nevertheless, the Motion is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to produce further responses to: Form Interrogatories 2.6, 6.7(b), 7.3, 9.1, 12.1 and 50.2-50.6 and Special Interrogatories 6, 19, 22, 23 and 26. Plaintiff is further ordered to produce responsive documents to Requests for Production of Documents 1-31. The Court orders counsel, Mark Hooshmand, to pay sanctions to Defendant of $2500.00. Payment, responses and responsive documents are due within 15 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHILOH FULL GOSPEL CHURCH et al VS. JOHN DEMATTEI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests Fro Production Of Documents, Requests For Admission And Request For Statement Of Damages; Request For Related Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 6, 2013, Line 7. DEFENDANT JOHN DEMATTEI'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests Fro Production Of Documents, Requests For Admission And Request For Statement Of Damages; Request For Related Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. The Court orders Plaintiff to produce responses to the Request for Statement of Damages and verified responses as to Form Interrogatory Nos. 1.1, 2.9, 2.10 and 14.1 within 15 days of this order. Defendant's motion as to the other discovery is denied. Defendant failed to comply with CRC, rule 3.1345 in not providing a separate statement, failed to meet and confer in good faith as required and filed an untimely motion to compel with regard to this discovery. No verification is required for responses with objections only. See CCP §§ 2030.250, 2031.250 and 2033.240. Finally, Food 4 Less Supermarkets, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 651, 657-658 states that "if the response is served within the statutory time period, that portion of the response must be considered timely notwithstanding the lack of verification. The omission of the verification in the portion of the response containing fact-specific responses merely renders that portion of the response untimely and therefore only creates a right to move for orders and sanctions under subdivision (k) of section 2031 as to those responses but does not result in a waiver of the objections made." Defendant provided actual responses as to Form Interrogatory Nos. 1.1, 2.9, 2.10 and 14.1 and therefore, verified responses are due as to those requests only. Finally, the Court denies the Plaintiff's requests for sanctions and orders the Plaintiff's counsel, Laura Flynn, to pay sanctions to Defendants in the amount of $2037.50 within 15 days of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD HARRIS VS. NATIONAL TENANT NETWORK, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant National Tenant Network, Inc.'S Motion To Strike Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 6, 2013, Line 18, Defendant National Tenant Network, Inc.'s Motion To Strike Complaint Denied, in light of and for the reasons set forth in overruling the demurrer. The Court does not conclude as a matter of law that, assuming the truth of the factual allegations, there is "no reasonable possibility that the requirements for class certification will be satisfied." Tucker v. Pacific Bell Mobile Services, 208 Cal. App. 4th 201, 211 (2012). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD HARRIS VS. NATIONAL TENANT NETWORK, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant National Tenant Network, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 6, 2013, Line 18, Defendant National Tenant Network, Inc.'s Demurrer To Complaint The demurrers are overruled. The Court does not conclude as a matter of law that, assuming the truth of the factual allegations, there is "no reasonable possibility that the requirements for class certification will be satisfied." Tucker v. Pacific Bell Mobile Services, 208 Cal. App. 4th 201, 211 (2012). As to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action, Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the ICCRA violations. As to the 4th and 5th causes of action, plaintiff has standing to pursue the UCL cause of action, and may be able to obtain injunctive relief. These causes of action are pled with sufficient particularity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
